seoTitle: How to Connect Clover POS to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Sync your Clover point-of-sale sales data into QuickBooks Online using a connector app, and automate daily summaries and fee tracking.
intro: Manually re-entering daily Clover sales totals into QuickBooks is a time sink and an easy place to make errors. This guide covers connecting Clover to QuickBooks Online through a marketplace connector app, so your sales, taxes, and fees sync automatically.

steps.heading: Open the QuickBooks Apps marketplace
steps.body: From the left-hand sidebar, scroll down and click Apps to browse available integrations.

steps.heading: Search for a Clover connector
steps.body: Use the search bar to find a trusted connector app like Commerce Sync, then select it from the results.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Click Get App Now and log in with your Clover administrator credentials to complete the secure authorization.

steps.heading: Choose your sync format
steps.body: Select whether you want a consolidated daily sales summary or individual line-item tracking, depending on how much detail you need.

steps.heading: Map your accounts
steps.body: Link Clover income, sales tax, and tips to the correct QuickBooks accounts, and assign a category for processing fees.

steps.heading: Test your sync
steps.body: Turn on the active synchronization switch and run a test sync to confirm your first daily summary appears correctly on your Profit & Loss report.

faq.question: Do I need a special app to connect Clover to QuickBooks?
faq.answer: Yes, since there's no native connection, you'll need a marketplace connector app like Commerce Sync or PayTraQer to link the two platforms.

faq.question: Will this track my processing fees separately?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign a dedicated expense category to automatically capture and group incoming merchant processing fees.

faq.question: Should I use daily summaries or individual transactions?
faq.answer: Consolidated daily summaries reduce clutter in your ledger, while individual line items give you more granular tracking, so the choice depends on your reporting needs.

faq.question: How do I know the sync is working correctly?
faq.answer: Run a test sync after setup and check that your Clover sales appear correctly on your Profit & Loss report before relying on it fully.
